exemplar.title: An investigation of the dual processing model in relation to decision making and extrapolation

detailedExemplarData.title: An investigation of the dual processing model in relation to decision making and extrapolation

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: The aim is clearly stated and its real-world relevance is convincingly explained with a business pricing example.
The dual-processing model is accurately described and explicitly linked to anchoring bias in the factorial task.
Both IVs and the DV are precisely operationalized and both null and directional hypotheses are presented correctly.
weaknesses: The business example, while effective, could be expanded to cover additional market contexts for greater depth.
There is a minor factual typo (median vs. mean in the historical study) that should be corrected for precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All introduction strands meet the top-band descriptors: the aim is not only stated but its relevance thoroughly explained; theory/model description and link to the task are explicit; hypotheses and variables are fully operationalized. Minor brevity in the example and a small factual slip are too slight to detract from a maximum mark.

strengths: The independent-measures design is justified by a clear rationale against demand characteristics.
Controlled variables are listed and each is explained with respect to minimizing confounds.
Participant characteristics and sampling technique are described with some justification of their selection.
weaknesses: Opportunity sampling is not critically evaluated for its impact on generalizability.
Justifications for some materials (e.g., timer app) are missing.
Discussion of how cultural background influences anchoring is underdeveloped.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration section consistently falls in the mid band: the design is explained (band 3-4), but sampling and materials explanations remain superficial. While one strand reaches the top of the band, the others do not justify a higher mark than 3.

strengths: Statistical findings are clearly stated with correct test choice (Mann-Whitney U) and linked back to hypotheses.
Interpretation of the results relates directly to the data and hypothesis, discussing acceptance of the null.
Descriptive measures (median, SIR) are justified for non-normal data.
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in p-value reporting (p < 0.05 vs p = 0.5) undermine clarity of inferential conclusions.
Graphical presentation lacks axis labels and clear units, reducing standalone interpretability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis is strong overall: appropriate descriptive and inferential statistics are applied and interpreted with respect to the hypothesis, meriting the top band score of 5 despite minor reporting and presentation errors. The depth of interpretation justifies a mark above the mid band.

strengths: Findings are discussed in relation to the dual-processing model and contrasted with the original study.
Strengths and limitations are clearly explained and tied to the investigationâs validity.
Modifications are explicitly linked to identified limitations and justified logically.
weaknesses: The discussion of theoretical implications remains largely descriptive rather than deeply analytical.
The evaluation section ends abruptly, suggesting an incomplete final synthesis of broader implications.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation demonstrates strong theoretical integration, well-explained strengths/limitations, and justified modifications, placing it solidly in the top band. A more thorough theoretical critique and a complete concluding synthesis would be needed to reach the absolute maximum.

strandSelections.reasoning: A box-and-whisker plot is an appropriate graph for medians and spread, directly comparing the two conditions.  Outliers and sample sizes are indicated, so the graph addresses the hypothesis.  Nonetheless, the axes are not fully visible in the submission excerpt and the captioning is rudimentary, suggesting **presentation errors**.  This aligns with the 3-4 band but at the lower score of 3.

The statistical results are **clearly stated** (U, p, decision on hypothesis).  The student then interprets these in relation to the data (no significant difference) and explicitly links the finding back to accepting the null and rejecting the research hypothesis.  Minor confusion in p-value wording prevents the absolute top mark, but the criteria for the highest band are essentially met, so a 5 is awarded.

Both IV conditions are **clearly named and operationalised** (ascending vs. descending factorial).  The DV is likewise operationalised as âmedian product estimateâ.  A directional and null hypothesis are both presented with correct reference to statistical significance.  Because every element is precise and fully operationalised, the strand attains the maximum score.

Sampling technique (opportunity) and participant characteristics (age, gender balance, cultural background, English fluency) are **outlined and partially justified** (time constraints, generalisability).  However, the explanation remains surface-level and does not fully evaluate how opportunity sampling affects representativeness.  This fits the lower end of the 3-4 band, so a 3 is allocated.

The dual-processing model is **accurately described** with characteristics of both systems, supported by citations.  The write-up then **explicitly links** the model to anchoring bias and the factorial task, explaining how limited time pushes participants from System 2 calculation to System 1 heuristic use.  This satisfies âdescribed and link explainedâ.  Minor factual slips (e.g., median typo in historical study) do not undermine the overall quality; hence a solid 5 in the top band.

The student **describes the findings in relation to the dual-processing model** and contrasts them with the original study.  However, the discussion remains largely descriptive; deeper theoretical implications or alternative explanations are only briefly touched upon.  This corresponds to âdescribed with referenceâ, earning the upper score of the 3-4 band.

The student clearly states the aim â to replicate Tversky and Kahneman (1974) and investigate the effect of anchoring bias on extrapolation â and then **explicitly explains why this aim matters**, giving a concrete example of how firms can use anchoring in pricing strategy.  The relevance is not merely identified but **logically linked to real-world application**, fulfilling the descriptor for the top band.  Minor brevity in the business example prevents a perfect 6, but the explanation is still strong enough for a high-band award.

Multiple strengths and limitations are **clearly explained** and each is tied to a concrete element of the investigation (e.g., order effects avoided, participant variability introduced).  Explanations show relevance to validity.  Minor repetition and the absence of quantitative consideration of impact leave slight room for improvement, so a 5 within the top band is justified.

The student lists several controlled variables (quiet room, strict 5-second exposure, rapport) and **explains why each is controlled** (distraction, social desirability, equal timing).  Materials are also listed.  The explanations are concise but adequate, meriting the 3-4 band; minor omissions (e.g., no justification for timer app) justify a mid-band score of 3.

For every limitation identified, the student proposes a **specific, justified modification** (e.g., larger sample to counter variability, pre-testing maths ability, stricter enforcement of 5-second limit).  The logical linkage between limitation and remedy is explicit.  Some suggestions could be elaborated with feasibility details, but criteria for the highest band are substantially met, so a score of 5 is awarded.

The student goes beyond listing the independent-measures design: they **justify** it by contrasting with repeated measures and describing potential demand characteristics.  This explanatory component matches the 3-4 band.  The rationale could be deeper (e.g., no discussion of between-group variability control), so a 4 is appropriate rather than a perfect 4.

Appropriate descriptive (median, SIR) and inferential (Mann-Whitney U) statistics are chosen given non-normal data and independent groups.  Calculations are shown and p-value reported.  There is, however, a questionable statement that z = 0 and a mismatch when citing p < 0.05 vs p = 0.5, indicating **minor accuracy errors**.  Hence the work fits the 3-4 band, earning a 4 for generally correct application with some mistakes.

annotations.comment: The historical study description is clear, but an explicit statement linking these findings to the current factorial task would strengthen the theoretical connection.
The aim is articulated clearly and linked to a real-world business application, illustrating strong relevance of anchoring bias to pricing strategies.
Rationale for international participants is noted but the discussion of how cultural backgrounds specifically influence anchoring or extrapolation is underdeveloped.
Controlled variables are identified with clear rationale, demonstrating strong control over environmental and social confounds.
The title is concise and clearly conveys the focus on the dual-processing model and extrapolation, effectively framing the investigation.
The student provides a detailed description of System 1 and System 2 with appropriate citations, demonstrating strong grasp of the dual-processing model.
Dependent variable is clearly operationalized as the median product estimate, providing precise measurement criteria.
The presentation of both null and directional hypotheses is precise, correctly naming IV conditions and linking to statistical significance.
The independent-measures design is well justified, with a clear rationale on why this limits demand characteristics in replication.
Opportunity sampling is described but lacks critical evaluation of how it may bias the sample and affect generalizability.
Consider elaborating on how variables like age distribution and teaching experience could introduce confounding effects on extrapolation.
The materials list is comprehensive but omits justification for choices like the timer app; clarify why each material was selected.
The rationale for using median and semi-interquartile range on non-normal data is articulated clearly and appropriately.
Reporting standard deviation for skewed data may confuse; focus on robust spread measures like semi-IQR in non-parametric contexts.
Table 1 succinctly displays medians and variability for each condition, enhancing clarity in comparing the two groups.
The box-and-whisker plot lacks axis labels and unit indicators, which reduces its self-containment and interpretability.
The choice of the Mann-Whitney U test is appropriate for independent measures and non-normal ratio data.
Include interpretation of effect size magnitude beyond statistical significance to convey practical implications of U = 118.5.
There is an inconsistency between stating p < 0.05 and reporting p = 0.5, which undermines the inferential conclusion and requires correction.
The discussion effectively links the studyâs findings back to the dual-processing model, demonstrating clear theoretical integration.
The evaluation section ends mid-argument regarding sample size limitations; the incomplete discussion reduces the depth of critical analysis.
Strengths and limitations are directly tied to validity concerns, showing thoughtful reflection on the investigationâs rigor.
To deepen evaluation, explicitly link each limitation to targeted modifications and discuss their feasibility.
The conclusion misstates the significance threshold (â¤ 0.05) when results were non-significant; revise this to align with actual p-value reporting.
Expand the conclusion to discuss broader theoretical implications of accepting the null and propose directions for future research.
